danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player character; encounter triggers combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Movement between map zones (cabins, grounds, cave)
danger_signals.meaning: Entering new danger areas; Jason spawns in different locations across game progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Pamela Voorhees' cave location with darker palette
danger_signals.meaning: Final danger zone; culmination of threat level

safety_signals.visual_form: Enclosed building spaces on camp map
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary shelter locations where Jason cannot immediately reach player

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapons and tools scattered throughout environment (machetes, knives, axes)
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to increase combat capability and survival chances

safety_signals.visual_form: Green-shaded pathways on camp map connecting locations
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal routes between zones; areas of navigational clarity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hockey mask and machete silhouette (film-derived iconic imagery)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Jason sprite behavior changes based on player proximity and game phase
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with cave location (Pamela Voorhees' lair); appears across camp grounds
Operate from cabin bases; occupy open camp terrain; attempt to reach safe zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Camp map visible with multiple unvisited locations; cabins appear intact and accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jason appears at lower frequency; player can navigate relatively freely to collect items and weapons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Camp becomes more dangerous as Jason patrols multiple zones; visual intimation of hunting across territory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jason encounters increase in frequency; combat becomes necessary; evasion tactics required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to cave environment with darker palette; Pamela Voorhees lair becomes visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jason becomes aggressive final boss; player must use collected weapons; cave represents point of no return

environmental_storytelling.example: Camp grounds → cabins → forest → cave represents player journey from safe space to source of threat (Pamela Voorhees' lair)
Weapons and tools scattered through environment suggest previous survivor attempts and resource scavenging

environmental_storytelling.technique: Palette shift to indicate danger zone
environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Dark palette in cave versus lighter camp exteriors communicates location threat level

environmental_storytelling.example: Hidden path details and palette-limited sprite overlays show intentional design to separate safe routes from dangerous territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spawns at multiple locations across game progression; patrol patterns escalate in frequency as player advances
Aggressive combat mode in cave location; boss-type encounter requiring weapon use and combat tactics
Can move freely through cabin and outdoor areas collecting items; exploration behavior possible
Must engage or evade Jason; behavior shifts from exploration to survival mode when Jason encounters

notes: Friday the 13th NES is an action game that converts slasher film narrative into gameplay. The game communicates danger primarily through location-based threat escalation (camp grounds → cave) and Jason spawn frequency increases. Safety is indicated through cabin shelter and weapon collection. The visual design uses palette limitations strategically: hidden path details in the camp map, sprite overlays for path shading, and darker colors for danger zones. Unused graphics (whistle, vial) suggest cut safety/utility mechanics. The game lacks traditional faction complexity but establishes killer-vs-survivors dichotomy through spatial design and enemy behavior patterns. World-state transitions are marked by location changes and threat intensity rather than narrative cinematics.
